_comment: Asymmetric cost of a staffing decision, per operative person-day, in EUR. Admin desks (a constant 8 person-days/day) are excluded from scoring — you always staff them. The scoring target each day is the realized operative need (= PRESENT_TOTAL - 8). See README.md section 'How decisions are scored' and SOLUTION/scoring.py for the reference implementation.

overstaffing._comment: Planned > needed: surplus people are paid but idle. Full wage wasted, no premium.

understaffing._comment: Planned < needed: the work still has to ship. The shortfall is covered by overtime / temp labour at a premium. A small shortfall is absorbed by the standing crew (cheap — only the premium); past the SLA tolerance, late trucks and missed carrier cut-offs trigger a steep penalty.

scoring_note: Excess cost vs. a perfect plan (planned == needed): overstaffing costs 230 EUR per surplus person-day; understaffing costs 41.4 EUR per short person-day (the 18% premium) PLUS 600 EUR per short person-day beyond the 2.0 tolerance. Consequence: trimming the optimiser's overstaffing pays off fast, but a small, deliberate undershoot is cheaper than a safe overshoot — until you cross the tolerance, where it explodes. Cut toward the truth, not past it.
